info.name: Subsystem Discovery Engine v3 (Codeanalyzer)
info.description:
Complete API collection for the Subsystem Discovery Engine.

Base URL  : http://localhost:8081/codeanalyzer/server
Context path is /codeanalyzer/server (set via server.servlet.context-path in application.yml).

Folders:
  1. DB-only (Production) – endpoints that load graph data from the database by applicationId
  2. Demo / Synthetic      – built-in example and synthetic graph endpoints (no DB needed)
  3. Transform only        – inspect the Leiden input without running the full algorithm

variable.description: Base URL including context path. Change host/port here to point at a different environment.
Numeric ID of the application in the Analyzer database (tb_node.application_id).
Human-readable key for the application (used as a cache key internally).

item.name: 1 – DB-only (Production) Endpoints
item.description: These three endpoints are the ones called by the Analyzer integration. They load node/relation graph data from the database using applicationId.

request.description:
Runs the full Leiden subsystem discovery algorithm using graph data loaded from the database.

Path variable:
  {applicationId} – e.g. 14

Body fields:
  graphTypes         – one or more of CALL_GRAPH, CODE_ASSOCIATION, HTTP_API, CLASS_DIAGRAM
  runs               – number of Leiden iterations (higher = more stable, slower)
  consensusThreshold – fraction of runs a node-cluster assignment must appear in (0–1)
  resolution         – Leiden resolution parameter (higher = smaller clusters)
  useLlmLabels       – set true to call OpenAI for subsystem names (requires OPENAI_API_KEY)

Returns: SubsystemDiscoveryResponse

Loads graph data from the database and returns the normalised Leiden input (nodes + weighted edges) WITHOUT running the Leiden algorithm. Useful for inspecting the weighted graph that would be fed into the algorithm.

Returns: LeidenInputDto { nodeCount, edgeCount, nodes[], edges[] }

item.name: Transform Inline JSON → Leiden Input – POST

request.description:
Transforms the provided request (either inline graphs in the body or DB-loaded data when graphs is null) into a LeidenInputDto. Useful for debugging the normalisation step.

Returns: LeidenInputDto { nodeCount, edgeCount, nodes[], edges[] }

item.name: 2 – Demo / Synthetic Graph Endpoints
item.description: Built-in example and synthetically generated graphs. No database required. Good for smoke-testing the algorithm.

request.description: Returns metadata for all hardcoded example graphs (e.g. analyzer-history). Use the returned 'key' value in the other example endpoints.
Runs full Leiden discovery on the hardcoded analyzer-history example graph. No body needed – parameters are passed as query strings.
Returns the pre-built Leiden input for the analyzer-history demo graph without running the algorithm.
Returns the raw RawGraphDto list for the analyzer-history demo (before normalisation).
Runs discovery on a synthetically generated graph with 100 nodes.
Runs discovery on a synthetically generated graph with 1000 nodes.
Runs discovery on a large synthetically generated graph with 10,000 nodes (1 run, no consensus – fastest).
Returns the Leiden input for the 100-node synthetic graph (no algorithm run).
Returns the raw generated graph with 100 nodes before normalisation.

item.name: 3 – Inline JSON Discovery (no DB)
item.description: Pass full graph JSON directly in the request body. Useful for one-off testing without a database.

request.description:
Runs full Leiden subsystem discovery using inline graph JSON. No database connection needed.

The 'graphs' array contains one or more RawGraphDto objects, each with:
  graphType     – CALL_GRAPH | CODE_ASSOCIATION | HTTP_API | CLASS_DIAGRAM
  nodeDataArray – list of node objects
  linkDataArray – list of edge objects
